text_blocks.text: Acknowledgements
[[strong]]Acknowledgements[[/strong]]

Since 2015 I tried many times to integrate my complex analysis notes with
                applets that I have designed or adapted from other people. Finally, in 2019,
                I was able to accomplish this task thanks to the development of mathematical,
                open-source, software capable to run online in (almost) any browser. This book
                would not have been possible without a ton of people sharing their great work.
                As much as possible, I've tried to keep track of all the online resources,
                books and articles that I have used.
A big [[strong]]thank you[[/strong]] to all the people who support, or have supported, this project:

[[strong]]Patreon:[[/strong]] 
                    Edward Huff,
                    Abei,
                    pmbem,
                    [[a href="https://twitter.com/fractalkitty" target="_blank"]]Sophia Wood[[/a]],
                    Adam Parrott,
                    Doug Kuhlman,
                    bleh,
                    Miguel Díaz,
                    Ruan Ramon,
                    Maciej Lasota,
                    Christopher-Alexander Hermanns,
                    Aarón Reyes,
                    Jerome Siegler,
                    Yashar Shoraka,
                    Shaun MacMillan, 
                    Scott Pedersen, 
                    Ihsan Karabulut,
                    Elias Sanchez Angarano,
                    Sarah Gelsinger Brewer,
                    F. Lengyel,
                    Thomas Smith.

[[strong]]GitHub Sponsors:[[/strong]]
                   erob409

Kelly E. Matthews,
                    Alex Iktan,
                    Codi Quetzal,
                    Feya,
                    [[a href="https://twitter.com/nicoguaro" target="_blank"]]Nicolás Guarín-Zapata[[/a]],
                    Carla Luciane Klôa Schöninger,
                    Ken Thele,
                    [[a href="https://twitter.com/mike_mates" target="_blank"]]Mates Mike[[/a]],
                    Christopher Lee,
                    [[a href="https://twitter.com/Alvy" target="_blank"]]Alvy[[/a]],
                    Jason Cunliffe,
                    Julian Miranda,
                    Leticia Hernández López,
                    SureshKumar M S,
                    [[a href="https://github.com/rchappa" target="_blank"]]Rodrigo Chappa[[/a]],
                    Fahim Ahmed,
                    [[a href="https://github.com/sprowell" target="_blank"]]Stacey Prowell[[/a]],
                    Eric Peper,
                    Paul St. Jean,
                    [[a href="https://twitter.com/a_busch" target="_blank"]]Antoine Büsch[[/a]],
                    Rose-Maree Locsei,
                    Michael Rivera,
                    Mengxuan Qiu,
                    Olympia Ellinas,
                    Simon,
                    Zoltán Köllő,
                    Jason Sabloff,
                    José Ramón Montejo Garai,
                    Philip Benjamin,
                    Anton Pirogov,
                    Luciano Merenda,
                    Wilson Sawyer,
                    Emiliano Espinosa,
                    [[a href="https://github.com/eamonsuo" target="_blank"]]eamounsou[[/a]],
                    Cameron Fredrickson,
                    Heather Moore,
                    [[a href="https://twitter.com/ArsoCivil" target="_blank"]]David Arso Civil[[/a]],
                    Juan Martinez
                    Juan Lopez.
Many thanks also to:

[[a href="http://www.jeffreythompson.org/" target="_blank"]]Jeffrey Thompson[[/a]].
                    The initial design of this site was inspired by his book [[a
                        href="http://www.jeffreythompson.org/collision-detection/index.php" target="_blank"]]Collision
                        Detection[[/a]]. If you want to learn about the
                    algorithms behind collisions using basic geometrical shapes, you should check it!

[[a href="https://geo.ma.tum.de/en/people/aaron-montag.html" target="_blank"]]Aaron Montag[[/a]], [[a
                        href="http://martin.von-gagern.net/" target="_blank"]]Martin von Gagern[[/a]], [[a
                        href="https://github.com/kranich" target="_blank"]]Stefan Kranich[[/a]] and [[a
                        href="https://github.com/strobelm" target="_blank"]]Michael Strobel[[/a]]
                    @@verbatim:1:0c74f9@@ creators, developers and
                    contributors of the [[a href="https://cindyjs.org/" target="_blank"]]CindyJS[[/a]] project. Thanks for
                    allowing me to adapt your great applets.

[[a href="http://www.science-to-touch.com/en/about.html" target="_blank"]]Jürgen Richter-Gebert[[/a]],
                    one of the authors of the interactive mathematics software [[a
                        href="https://www.cinderella.de/tiki-index.php" target="_blank"]]Cinderella[[/a]], who now works on
                    the [[a href="https://cindyjs.org/" target="_blank"]]CindyJS[[/a]] project.

The [[a href="https://www.geogebra.org/" target="_blank"]]GeoGebra[[/a]] developers and community who
                    share their wonderful constructions and applets.

[[a href="https://shiffman.net/" target="_blank"]]Daniel Shiffman[[/a]], an amazing teacher who shares
                    his knowledge with the world. I have learned all the basics about programming from his [[a
                        href="https://shiffman.net/books/"]]books[[/a]] and video tutorials [[a
                        href="https://thecodingtrain.com/"]]The Coding Train[[/a]].

[[a href="http://lauren-mccarthy.com/about" target="_blank"]]Lauren McCarthy[[/a]], the creator of [[a
                        href="https://p5js.org/" target="_blank"]]p5.js[[/a]], and to the great community of creative
                    people who use this programming language.

[[a href="https://paulmasson.github.io/" target="_blank"]]Paul Masson[[/a]], the creator of [[a
                        href="http://mathcell.org/" target="_blank"]]MathCell[[/a]]. This is JavaScript library for
                    including interactive mathematics in a web browser.

The equations in the book are displayed thanks to [[a href="https://www.mathjax.org/"]]MathJax[[/a]].
                    However, in some applets [[a href="https://katex.org/" target="_blank"]]KaTeX[[/a]] is used instead.
Finally, about the applets:

The applets "Analytic Landscapes" and "Taylor Series" were adapted for the purposes of this book.
                    The original versions can be found in the [[a
                        href="https://cindyjs.org/gallery/cindygl/"]]CindyGL-Gallery[[/a]].

I designed all the GeoGebra applets. They can be downloaded from this [[a
                        href="https://www.geogebra.org/m/m8qcakxd" target="_blank"]]GeoGebra book[[/a]].

I also designed all the p5.js applets and the source code can be found at [[a
                        href="https://github.com/complex-analysis/complex-analysis.github.io/tree/master/applets/p5js"
                        target="_blank"]]GitHub[[/a]].
Reader's comments [[i class="fa-solid fa-angles-right"]][[/i]]

_protected_html:
@@verbatim:0:743535@@

Acknowledgements

Acknowledgements

Since 2015 I tried many times to integrate my complex analysis notes with applets that I have designed or adapted from other people. Finally, in 2019, I was able to accomplish this task thanks to the development of mathematical, open-source, software capable to run online in (almost) any browser. This book would not have been possible without a ton of people sharing their great work. As much as possible, I've tried to keep track of all the online resources, books and articles that I have used.

A big thank you to all the people who support, or have supported, this project:

Patreon: Edward Huff, Abei, pmbem, Sophia Wood, Adam Parrott, Doug Kuhlman, bleh, Miguel Díaz, Ruan Ramon, Maciej Lasota, Christopher-Alexander Hermanns, Aarón Reyes, Jerome Siegler, Yashar Shoraka, Shaun MacMillan, Scott Pedersen, Ihsan Karabulut, Elias Sanchez Angarano, Sarah Gelsinger Brewer, F. Lengyel, Thomas Smith.

GitHub Sponsors: erob409

Kelly E. Matthews, Alex Iktan, Codi Quetzal, Feya, Nicolás Guarín-Zapata, Carla Luciane Klôa Schöninger, Ken Thele, Mates Mike, Christopher Lee, Alvy, Jason Cunliffe, Julian Miranda, Leticia Hernández López, SureshKumar M S, Rodrigo Chappa, Fahim Ahmed, Stacey Prowell, Eric Peper, Paul St. Jean, Antoine Büsch, Rose-Maree Locsei, Michael Rivera, Mengxuan Qiu, Olympia Ellinas, Simon, Zoltán Köllő, Jason Sabloff, José Ramón Montejo Garai, Philip Benjamin, Anton Pirogov, Luciano Merenda, Wilson Sawyer, Emiliano Espinosa, eamounsou, Cameron Fredrickson, Heather Moore, David Arso Civil, Juan Martinez Juan Lopez.

Many thanks also to:

  • Jeffrey Thompson. The initial design of this site was inspired by his book Collision Detection. If you want to learn about the algorithms behind collisions using basic geometrical shapes, you should check it!
  • Aaron Montag, Martin von Gagern, Stefan Kranich and Michael Strobel @@verbatim:1:0c74f9@@ creators, developers and contributors of the CindyJS project. Thanks for allowing me to adapt your great applets.
  • Jürgen Richter-Gebert, one of the authors of the interactive mathematics software Cinderella, who now works on the CindyJS project.
  • The GeoGebra developers and community who share their wonderful constructions and applets.
  • Daniel Shiffman, an amazing teacher who shares his knowledge with the world. I have learned all the basics about programming from his books and video tutorials The Coding Train.
  • Lauren McCarthy, the creator of p5.js, and to the great community of creative people who use this programming language.
  • Paul Masson, the creator of MathCell. This is JavaScript library for including interactive mathematics in a web browser.
  • The equations in the book are displayed thanks to MathJax. However, in some applets KaTeX is used instead.

Finally, about the applets:

  • The applets "Analytic Landscapes" and "Taylor Series" were adapted for the purposes of this book. The original versions can be found in the CindyGL-Gallery.
  • I designed all the GeoGebra applets. They can be downloaded from this GeoGebra book.
  • I also designed all the p5.js applets and the source code can be found at GitHub.

Reader's comments
